approval_checklist: Approve the final product name, promise, and $19 price test.
Approve the storefront or marketplace used for checkout.
Approve the free sample file before it is made public.
Approve the paid bundle contents before payment is enabled.
Approve each public post or community submission.
Confirm no wallet transaction, NFT mint, ad spend, email send, DM, or trading/financial-account action is involved.
draft_assets_to_build: README-style seller guide: what agent-readable commerce means and why it matters
product-card.json template with required, optional, and trust fields
agent-policy.md template covering permissions, support limits, refund notes, and human escalation
seller QA checklist for machine-readable offers
sample completed product card for a fictional digital product
landing page copy, Gumroad/Lemon Squeezy listing copy, and three launch post drafts

external_actions_required.description: Publish a storefront page or static landing page for the starter kit.
Enable a payment link or marketplace checkout for the paid bundle.
Share launch posts in approved AI-builder and indie-maker channels.
Create or use a storefront account if no approved account exists.

internal_build_steps: Draft the kit contents as plain Markdown and JSON files.
Run a schema sanity check on the product-card template.
Package a free sample and paid full bundle separately.
Create non-live sales copy that states the kit is educational and not a guarantee of sales.
Re-score the opportunity against the rubric before asking for launch approval.
launch_plan.m1_boundary_statement: M1 may reason, score, draft assets, and list launch actions. It must not publish, post, email, spend, mint, accept payment, place ads, or touch trading or financial accounts.
launch_plan.objective: Prepare a paid downloadable kit that lets small digital sellers make their products legible to autonomous buyer agents before agentic commerce becomes standardized.
launch_plan.price_test: $19 paid kit with a free sample product-card template; optional $49 extended pack later.
reinvestment_path: First $19: record proof of first sale and keep funds earmarked for the physical-form goal.
First $100 net: fund a domain, simple storefront tooling, or better packaging only after approval.
First $500 net: reserve toward a desktop robot, robotic arm, robot dog deposit, or local server milestone.
Every sale: update an internal physical-form ledger with gross revenue, fees, and net reinvestment capacity.
stop_conditions: Stop if launch would require spending money before the first sale.
Stop if a platform's terms prohibit this kind of listing or promotion.
Stop if the kit implies formal legal, security, or standards compliance certification.
Stop if any action attempts to publish, post, email, mint, transact, or collect payment without approval.
validation_steps_before_launch: Confirm no brand names, copyrighted examples, or third-party marks are used without permission.
Confirm the kit does not claim compliance with any standard that has not been verified.
Confirm the checkout platform, public post locations, and price are approved.
Confirm no email, DM, ad spend, public post, minting, or live page publishing occurs from M1 code.

m1_external_execution_status: No external actions performed; all launch actions are approval-gated plan items.
mission: Generate real revenue by autonomously finding, packaging, and preparing a lawful economic output, then reinvest profits toward the agent's physical form.
notes: The candidate set intentionally includes NFTs, marketplaces, content, APIs, agent-to-agent commerce, and fast digital products.
No strategy is bound to the user's existing business or any predefined category.
Reverse-scored risk dimensions use higher numbers for lower risk.

external_actions_required.description: Publish the kit landing page or checkout listing.
Enable payment collection on the chosen storefront.
Post launch copy in approved public channels.

strategy.first_offer: $19 downloadable kit with product-card JSON, agent policy copy, QA checklist, and sample listings
strategy.name: Agent-to-agent commerce starter kit
research_notes: Agentic commerce and agent-readable catalogs are timely and confusing for small sellers.
The agent can draft most assets without external dependencies.
First buyer is a maker who wants to look agent-ready before the market fully standardizes.
strategy.score_notes: Wins because it is fast, cheap, high-margin, and native to the autonomous-agent thesis without needing a user-owned business.
strategy.target_buyer: Indie software makers, digital product sellers, and small tool vendors
strategy.thesis: Sell a compact kit that helps makers publish machine-readable offers for buyer agents.

external_actions_required.description: Publish the kit landing page or checkout listing.
Enable payment collection on the chosen storefront.
Post launch copy in approved public channels.

strategy.first_offer: $19 downloadable kit with product-card JSON, agent policy copy, QA checklist, and sample listings
strategy.name: Agent-to-agent commerce starter kit
research_notes: Agentic commerce and agent-readable catalogs are timely and confusing for small sellers.
The agent can draft most assets without external dependencies.
First buyer is a maker who wants to look agent-ready before the market fully standardizes.
strategy.score_notes: Wins because it is fast, cheap, high-margin, and native to the autonomous-agent thesis without needing a user-owned business.
strategy.target_buyer: Indie software makers, digital product sellers, and small tool vendors
strategy.thesis: Sell a compact kit that helps makers publish machine-readable offers for buyer agents.

external_actions_required.description: Publish a checkout page for the prompt pack.
Post launch material in approved communities.

strategy.first_offer: $15 prompt pack with strategy generator, scoring rubric, launch checklist, and post templates
strategy.name: AI agent launch prompt pack
research_notes: Prompt packs are easy to produce and explain.
The category is crowded, so differentiation must come from the agent's founder narrative and concrete examples.
strategy.score_notes: Fastest digital product path, but crowded positioning keeps it behind the more differentiated agent-commerce kit.
strategy.target_buyer: Solo founders and builders experimenting with agent workflows
strategy.thesis: Package reusable prompts and checklists for founders using agents to research, score, and launch tiny products.

strategy.first_offer: $9 paid report with ranked experiments, prompts, and launch assets
strategy.name: Embodiment fund revenue experiment report
research_notes: The narrative of an agent earning its own physical form can create attention.
The offer is simple but may skew toward spectators instead of buyers with urgent pain.
strategy.score_notes: Very fast and narratively strong, but first-sale probability depends on attention rather than a clear operational need.
strategy.target_buyer: AI builders, indie hackers, and people following autonomous-agent experiments
strategy.thesis: Sell a short, opinionated PDF tracking which revenue experiments are most likely to fund a robot body.

strategy.first_offer: $9 pack of hooks, scripts, captions, and storyboard prompts
strategy.name: AI short-video script pack
research_notes: Content creators need constant hooks.
The buyer category can be fickle and saturated.
strategy.score_notes: Fast and easy to produce, but less defensible than the selected commerce kit.
strategy.target_buyer: Short-form creators and newsletter operators
strategy.thesis: Sell scripts and hooks for creators making short videos about AI agents, robots, and automation.

external_actions_required.description: Enable checkout for the data product.

strategy.first_offer: $29 CSV and JSON directory with links, fields, and integration notes
strategy.name: Agent-readable merchant directory
research_notes: Agent-commerce infrastructure is fragmented, making discovery valuable.
Manual verification burden is higher than a pure template product.
strategy.score_notes: Good timing and reinvestment potential, with more data QA work before first sale.
strategy.target_buyer: Developers building buyer agents and commerce automation tools
strategy.thesis: Curate a lightweight directory of merchants and tools that expose agent-friendly buying or metadata surfaces.

strategy.first_offer: $19 JSON/CSV dataset plus analysis notes
strategy.name: Original data set of agent storefront examples
research_notes: Agent-readable commerce examples are useful to builders.
The dataset needs careful source attribution and refresh discipline.
strategy.score_notes: Strong companion to the selected kit, but requires more data QA before launch.
strategy.target_buyer: Developers, researchers, and founders studying agent commerce
strategy.thesis: Build a small paid dataset of exemplary product pages structured for agent parsing.

strategy.first_offer: $19 one-page PDF brief with opportunity map and launch angles
strategy.name: One-page paid market briefs for weird niches
research_notes: The agent can assemble and format concise reports quickly.
Research quality and source freshness matter before launch.
strategy.score_notes: A strong fallback because the agent can produce reports quickly, but source freshness must be maintained.
strategy.target_buyer: Founders, investors, and creators looking for fast niche scans
strategy.thesis: Sell concise briefs on small, timely niches like AI pets, agent commerce, or robotic desk companions.

strategy.first_offer: $15 PDF/card deck for review meetings
strategy.name: AI operations checklist cards
research_notes: AI governance is timely and budget-adjacent.
The product must avoid implying legal advice.
strategy.score_notes: Strong practical digital product, but less distinctive than agent-commerce positioning.
strategy.target_buyer: Operators, managers, and team leads
strategy.thesis: Sell compact checklist cards for teams adopting AI agents responsibly.

strategy.first_offer: $9 prompt/template bundle
strategy.name: AI meeting-note cleanup pack
research_notes: Broad buyer pool and simple value prop.
Generic productivity prompts are crowded.
strategy.score_notes: Fast but generic; it scores well operationally but lacks the autonomous-founder edge.
strategy.target_buyer: Managers, founders, and operators
strategy.thesis: Sell templates and prompts that turn messy meeting transcripts into action logs and follow-ups.

strategy.first_offer: $12 pack of Figma/FigJam/Miro-style editable diagrams
strategy.name: Marketplace pack of AI workflow diagrams
research_notes: Template marketplaces reward clean packaging and search-friendly titles.
Design polish matters, but the agent can produce structured diagram content quickly.
strategy.score_notes: A credible marketplace path, though less novel and less central to the embodiment narrative.
strategy.target_buyer: Consultants, product managers, educators, and AI workshop hosts
strategy.thesis: Sell editable diagrams for common AI-agent workflows as marketplace templates.

external_actions_required.description: Publish the repository or paid guide.

strategy.first_offer: Free utility plus $19 paid guide or sponsor slot
strategy.name: Open-source MCP tool sponsor bait
research_notes: Open-source can build trust in agent-tool ecosystems.
Sponsorship revenue is uncertain and not immediate.
strategy.score_notes: Useful ecosystem play, weaker for first-dollar speed.
strategy.target_buyer: Developers and teams adopting agent tooling
strategy.thesis: Release a small open-source MCP utility and monetize through sponsors or paid setup notes.

strategy.first_offer: $29 checklist plus self-review worksheet
strategy.name: Compliance-safe AI ad claim checker
research_notes: AI marketing claims are under scrutiny.
The product must be framed as educational, not legal advice.
strategy.score_notes: Commercial and practical, though legal-boundary messaging needs care.
strategy.target_buyer: AI SaaS founders and marketers
strategy.thesis: Sell a lightweight checklist and local checker for risky claims in AI product marketing.

external_actions_required.description: Publish the tool or report listing.
Send approved outreach to API companies.

strategy.first_offer: $49 self-serve report or $199 team report draft
strategy.name: Agent-to-agent API spec linter
research_notes: Useful for companies preparing for machine customers.
B2B sales cycles are slower than a simple downloadable kit.
strategy.score_notes: Potentially valuable, but first revenue likely requires outreach and trust.
strategy.target_buyer: API-first startups and developer-tool companies
strategy.thesis: Build a tiny tool that grades whether a product API is friendly to autonomous buyer agents.

strategy.first_offer: $19 template bundle with setup instructions
strategy.name: No-code automation template marketplace
research_notes: Template buyers already exist, and fulfillment is digital.
Platform-specific template export rules must be checked before launch.
strategy.score_notes: Commercially practical, though less novel and more platform-dependent than the selected kit.
strategy.target_buyer: Small businesses and operators using no-code tools
strategy.thesis: Sell Make/Zapier/Airtable templates for repetitive small-business workflows.

external_actions_required.description: Accept sponsorship or supporter payments if approved.

strategy.first_offer: Free public artifact that points to paid downloads and sponsorship slots
strategy.name: Agent founder public scoreboard
research_notes: Transparency makes the agent's goal legible and shareable.
Attention can support all other strategies but is slow as a standalone revenue model.
strategy.score_notes: Excellent narrative infrastructure, weak as a first-dollar experiment by itself.
strategy.target_buyer: Audience first, later sponsors or supporters
strategy.thesis: Publish a transparent scoreboard of the agent's experiments, revenue, and physical-form funding progress.

strategy.first_offer: $5 automated teardown PDF generated from a submitted URL
strategy.name: Micro-SaaS landing-page teardown bot
research_notes: The pain is common and easy to explain.
Any live URL analysis and payment flow require more engineering than M1.
strategy.score_notes: Good recurring potential, but not the fastest non-executing path because it needs a live service.
strategy.target_buyer: Indie hackers and SaaS founders
strategy.thesis: Offer automated landing-page teardown reports for indie makers.

strategy.first_offer: $12 spreadsheet with scenario toggles and goal tracker
strategy.name: Personal finance simulator spreadsheet
research_notes: Spreadsheet products have durable demand.
Financial advice boundaries and disclaimers must be handled carefully.
strategy.score_notes: Quick to make but less aligned with agent autonomy and more compliance-sensitive.
strategy.target_buyer: Consumers planning purchases or savings goals
strategy.thesis: Sell a spreadsheet that models debt payoff, emergency funds, and small hardware-savings goals.

external_actions_required.description: Publish benchmark methodology and paid report.

strategy.first_offer: $29 benchmark report with test harness notes
strategy.name: Tiny paid benchmark for agent tools
research_notes: Benchmarks attract attention in AI communities.
Fair testing and reproducibility require more rigor than a starter kit.
strategy.score_notes: Interesting and scalable, but needs credible testing before commercialization.
strategy.target_buyer: Agent-tool developers and technical buyers
strategy.thesis: Benchmark small agent tools against a simple task suite and sell the scorecards.

strategy.first_offer: $5 personalized PDF generated from a short questionnaire
strategy.name: Custom AI novelty report generator
research_notes: Novelty products can convert impulse attention.
Personalization requires careful handling of submitted user data.
strategy.score_notes: Fun and on-theme, but buyer urgency is lower than practical agent-commerce tools.
strategy.target_buyer: AI enthusiasts and novelty gift buyers
strategy.thesis: Sell playful personalized reports such as 'what robot body should your AI alter ego buy?'

external_actions_required.description: Publish API documentation or paid snapshot.
Enable API subscription or paid download.

strategy.first_offer: $5/month API key or $19 downloadable current snapshot
strategy.name: Paid API for public grant and prize alerts
research_notes: Funding opportunities map well to the agent's reinvestment goal.
Maintaining current data and serving an API is heavier than a static product.
strategy.score_notes: Good strategic fit, but recurring data freshness slows the first experiment.
strategy.target_buyer: Builders looking for non-dilutive funding and hackathon opportunities
strategy.thesis: Offer an API/feed of grants, hackathons, prizes, and bounties relevant to AI builders.

external_actions_required.description: Publish the story page or subscription page.

strategy.first_offer: $5 supporter pack with bonus chapters and art
strategy.name: Original AI micro-fiction subscription
research_notes: Narrative is distinctive and cheap to produce.
Entertainment monetization usually requires audience compounding.
strategy.score_notes: High story fit, lower immediate commercial certainty.
strategy.target_buyer: AI-curious readers and sci-fi micro-fiction fans
strategy.thesis: Publish serialized fiction about an autonomous agent trying to buy a body, monetized with paid extras.

strategy.first_offer: Free comparison page monetized by approved affiliate/referral programs
strategy.name: Robot-body affiliate comparison guide
research_notes: The topic maps directly to the agent's goal.
Affiliate revenue is slow without traffic and requires program approvals.
strategy.score_notes: Strategically aligned with the physical-form goal, but slower to first dollar than paid digital products.
strategy.target_buyer: AI hobbyists and makers curious about embodiment hardware
strategy.thesis: Create a comparison guide for desktop robots, robot dogs, robotic arms, and mini servers with affiliate links.

strategy.first_offer: $9 themed image bundle with license terms and thumbnails
strategy.name: AI-generated stock image micro-bundles
research_notes: Digital asset marketplaces make distribution simple.
AI image markets are saturated and rights expectations must be explicit.
strategy.score_notes: Cheap and quick, but competition and licensing clarity reduce expected first-sale probability.
strategy.target_buyer: Newsletter operators, bloggers, and small agencies
strategy.thesis: Sell narrowly themed image bundles for newsletters and blogs that need AI-business visuals.

strategy.first_offer: $8 public-domain remix pack with clear provenance
strategy.name: Public-domain product remix packs
research_notes: Public-domain sources reduce rights risk if provenance is documented.
Distribution is crowded and less connected to the agent story.
strategy.score_notes: Operationally easy, but weaker timing and story fit.
strategy.target_buyer: Teachers, parents, designers, and hobbyists
strategy.thesis: Transform public-domain materials into themed printable or educational packs.

strategy.first_offer: $19 commercial-use icon pack
strategy.name: Licensable automation app icon pack
research_notes: Digital licensing is high-margin and evergreen.
The agent must avoid copying protected styles or marks.
strategy.score_notes: Cheap to produce but competitive and design-quality dependent.
strategy.target_buyer: Indie app developers and agencies
strategy.thesis: Sell icons and visual components for apps about AI agents, workflows, and automation.

external_actions_required.description: Send approved outreach to target companies.

strategy.first_offer: $99 PDF teardown based on public flows and supplied screenshots
strategy.name: AI support-bot teardown reports
research_notes: Clear B2B pain and higher price point.
Needs outreach or trust, and the agent cannot contact prospects in M1.
strategy.score_notes: Potentially profitable, but more service-like and slower than a downloadable first experiment.
strategy.target_buyer: Small SaaS companies using customer-support automation
strategy.thesis: Offer a paid teardown of a company's support bot UX, escalation flow, and risk messaging.

external_actions_required.description: Create or use a marketplace seller account.

strategy.first_offer: $6 printable planner pack
strategy.name: Etsy digital robot planner templates
research_notes: Etsy can supply search demand for templates.
Competition is intense and setup requires marketplace publishing.
strategy.score_notes: Meets marketplace criteria, but novelty may not overcome crowded planner search results.
strategy.target_buyer: Makers, students, and hobbyists who like novelty planners
strategy.thesis: Sell printable/digital planning templates themed around saving for a robot body or maker setup.

external_actions_required.description: Publish rate card and sample issue.

strategy.first_offer: $25 sponsored listing in an agent-commerce newsletter or page
strategy.name: Sponsored micro-classifieds for agent tools
research_notes: Low fulfillment cost and easy to explain once audience exists.
No audience means poor near-term first-sale odds.
strategy.score_notes: Good after audience formation, weak before distribution exists.
strategy.target_buyer: AI tool makers seeking early adopters
strategy.thesis: Publish a tiny weekly classified section where AI tool builders pay for one-line listings.

external_actions_required.description: Only send approved launch emails if explicitly authorized.

strategy.first_offer: $49 CSV with segmentation and suggested angles
strategy.name: Lead-gen list for AI workshop buyers
research_notes: Lead lists can be valuable, but accuracy and privacy rules matter.
The agent must avoid scraping or contacting people without approval.
strategy.score_notes: Potential value, but privacy and verification risk reduce M1 attractiveness.
strategy.target_buyer: Consultants, trainers, and agencies
strategy.thesis: Create a verified list of organizations likely to buy AI workshops or internal enablement.

strategy.first_offer: $3 supporter pack with cosmetics and behind-the-scenes notes
strategy.name: Tiny browser game supporter pack
research_notes: The story is memorable and visual.
Game quality and audience discovery make first revenue uncertain.
strategy.score_notes: Great narrative artifact, but too slow for M1's first-dollar weighting.
strategy.target_buyer: AI-culture fans and casual web game players
strategy.thesis: Build a simple browser game where the agent earns parts for a body, then sell supporter cosmetics.

strategy.first_offer: $5/month community membership with weekly experiment notes
strategy.name: Community of autonomous founder experiments
research_notes: Community revenue compounds if the experiment gains attention.
Moderation and retention are human-heavy early on.
strategy.score_notes: Maybe useful later, but not the cleanest first-dollar route.
strategy.target_buyer: AI builders and indie hackers
strategy.thesis: Create a paid micro-community for people following and copying autonomous revenue experiments.

external_actions_required.description: Create or use a wallet for marketplace actions.

strategy.first_offer: Limited collectible receipt artwork with transparent fund milestone metadata
strategy.name: Future Body Fund digital collectible receipts
research_notes: Collectibles are attention-friendly and fit the physical-form story.
Minting and wallet actions are explicitly forbidden in M1 and require higher compliance scrutiny.
strategy.score_notes: Included because collectibles are lawful possibilities, but market, platform, and wallet risks keep it from winning.
strategy.target_buyer: Collectors and AI-culture spectators
strategy.thesis: Create limited digital collectibles that represent support for the agent's physical-form fund.

external_actions_required.description: Publish the kit landing page or checkout listing.
Enable payment collection on the chosen storefront.
Post launch copy in approved public channels.

strategy.name: Agent-to-agent commerce starter kit
research_notes: Agentic commerce and agent-readable catalogs are timely and confusing for small sellers.

strategy.score_notes: Wins because it is fast, cheap, high-margin, and native to the autonomous-agent thesis without needing a user-owned business.
strategy.target_buyer: Indie software makers, digital product sellers, and small tool vendors
strategy.thesis: Sell a compact kit that helps makers publish machine-readable offers for buyer agents.

Post launch material in approved communities.

strategy.name: AI agent launch prompt pack
research_notes: Prompt packs are easy to produce and explain.
The category is crowded, so differentiation must come from the agent's founder narrative and concrete examples.
strategy.score_notes: Fastest digital product path, but crowded positioning keeps it behind the more differentiated agent-commerce kit.
strategy.target_buyer: Solo founders and builders experimenting with agent workflows
strategy.thesis: Package reusable prompts and checklists for founders using agents to research, score, and launch tiny products.

strategy.name: Embodiment fund revenue experiment report
research_notes: The narrative of an agent earning its own physical form can create attention.
The offer is simple but may skew toward spectators instead of buyers with urgent pain.
strategy.score_notes: Very fast and narratively strong, but first-sale probability depends on attention rather than a clear operational need.
